module.name: alert
module.description: Colored callout banner with a leading status glyph; the block body is the message (Markdown allowed) and the type parameter picks the severity.
module.aiSpec:
Draw a colored callout banner with a leading icon to flag a message as info, success, warning, or danger. Block module: the body (between the tag and `<!-- @end -->`) is the message and may be ordinary markdown. The only parameter is `type` (select: info | success | warning | danger, default info) — it sets both the border color and the glyph. Use for cautions, tips, or status notes; for a speech-style quote with a speaker use @balloon instead.
Example: <!-- @alert type: warning --> Back up the workspace **before** running the migration. <!-- @end -->

param.name: type
param.label: Type
param.desc: Alert severity, sets color and leading glyph

snippet.label: Alert (info)
snippet.text: <!-- @alert type: info --> This is an informational message. <!-- @end -->

snippet.label: Alert (success)
snippet.text: <!-- @alert type: success --> The operation completed successfully. <!-- @end -->

snippet.label: Alert (warning)
snippet.text: <!-- @alert type: warning --> Be careful with this action. <!-- @end -->

snippet.label: Alert (danger)
snippet.text: <!-- @alert type: danger --> This action is destructive and cannot be undone. <!-- @end -->
